IBAN / Account number
fields.help: Use account number or IBAN (International Bank Account Number). IBAN identifies the country, bank, branch and account for international payments. If you're having trouble with a number, try our [IBAN checker](https://wise.com/gb/iban/).

fields.label: SWIFT / BIC code
fields.help: 8-11 character code identifying the bank, country, city and branch. If you're having trouble with a code, try our [Swift / BIC checker](https://wise.com/swift-codes/).

fields.label: Full name of the account holder
Institution number
fields.help: The institution number is a 3-digit number identifying the financial institution to which a payment item is directed. Along with the account number, it is essential for delivering payments through the clearing system. [If you don't know the institution number of the beneficiary bank please try to locate it here.](https://wise.com/ca/bank-codes/transit-number/institution-number)

fields.help: You can start entering branch name in Kanji, Katakana or Romaji (English). If you can't see your bank in the list while typing in English, try entering your branch code instead.

branches.name: SWIFT
branches.alert: To send money to this account, we need to use the SWIFT network. It's slower, so the transfer might take 2-5 days longer. Also note that intermediary banks and your recipient's bank might charge extra fees to the amount received.

fields.help: CLABE (Clave Bancaria Estandarizada) is the standard format for bank account numbers in Mexico. A bank account's CLABE code is 18 digits long.

fields.label: CURP (optional)
fields.help: In order to speed up your transfer we recommend you to fill this field. The CURP (Clave Única de Registro de Población) is a unique registration number for private persons in Mexico.

fields.help: DuitNow is a unique ID to send money to Malaysian residents without knowing their bank details. Wise lets you send DuitNow transfers to mobile phone numbers (+60 12346789) or MyKad ID numbers (123456-12-1234)
